name: Ambuyat
origin: Sarawak, Sabah and Labuan (Popular in Brunei)
description: Replacement of rice. A staple food of the indigenous people of Sabah and Sarawak including Lundayeh/Lun Bawang.

name: Bee Hoon
description: A thin form of rice noodles (rice vermicelli).

name: Pulut
description: It is made from sticky rice.

name: Ketupat
description: It is made from rice packed inside a diamond-shaped container of woven palm leaf pouch.

name: Kway teow
description: A type of noodle made from rice with sprawl-shaped.

name: Lemang
description: A traditional Malay food made of glutinous rice, coconut milk and salt, cooked in a hollowed bamboo stick lined with banana leaves in order to prevent the rice from sticking to the bamboo.

name: Mi
description: Food made from unleavened dough which is rolled flat and cut, stretched or extruded, into long strips or strings.

name: Nasi putih
description: Steamed rice as a staple food.

name: Name
description: Description

name: Ayam bakar
description: Charcoal-grilled spiced chicken.

name: Ayam goreng
description: Spiced chicken fried in coconut oil.

name: Ayam kecap
description: Chicken simmered or braised in sweet soy sauce.

name: Ayam percik
description: Grilled chicken with a spicy, curry-like sauce.

name: Ayam tandori
description: Roasted chicken marinated in yoghurt and spices in a tandoor.

name: Bean Sprouts Chicken
description: Served with light soya sauce flavoured with oil.

name: Chai tow kway
description: A variety of dim sum.

name: Chilli crab
description: Derived from Chinese cuisine.

name: Curry
description: Derived from Indian cuisine.

name: Fish ball
description: Small balls that were made from fish.

name: Gulai
description: Meat cooked in a thick, rich and spicy curry sauce.

name: Ikan Bakar
description: Usually eaten with sambal or shrimp paste.

name: Ikan goreng
description: Spiced fish/seafood deep-fried in coconut oil

name: Kari ayam
description: A type of curry dish cooked using chicken and spices.

name: Kari kambing
description: A type of curry dish cooked using lamb or mutton.

name: Kari kepala ikan
description: A type of curry dish cooked using head of a red snapper, influenced by Indian and Peranakan cuisine.

name: Begedil
description: Derived from Javanese cuisine of Indonesia. Made of ground potatoes, minced meat, peeled and ground corn or tofu, or minced fish. Most common perkedel are made from mashed potatoes.

name: Rendang
description: Chunks of beef stewed in coconut milk and spicy thick curry gravy.

name: Telur pindang
description: Hardboiled eggs boiled in water mixed with salt, shallot skins, teak leaf and other spices.

name: Tempoyak ikan patin
origin: Northern and East Coast of Peninsular
description: Catfish served in sweet and spicy fermented durian curry.

name: Otak-otak
type: Cake type made from fish meat and spices
description: Traditionally served steamed, wrapped inside a banana leaf or grilled in coconut leaves.

name: Oyster omelette
description: The dish consists of an omelette with a filling primarily composed of small oysters.

name: Sata
description: Consisting of spiced fish meat wrapped in banana leaves and cooked on a grill.

name: Satay
description: Can be found throughout all the states of Malaysia in restaurants and on the street.

name: Satay celup
description: Popular in Malacca and Penang.

name: Name
description: Description

name: Sup Kambing
description: Can be found all over Malaysia.

name: Sayur Lodeh
description: Vegetable soup prepared in coconut milk

name: Yong tau foo
description: A type of Chinese soup dish.

name: Ayam pansuh
description: A chicken soup cooked in bamboo along with seasonings. A dish popular in Sarawak, Malaysia.

name: Bak kut teh
description: A soup that consists of meaty pork ribs in a complex broth of herbs and spices, boiled together with pork bones for hours.

name: Name
description: Description

name: Apam
description: A steamed dough made of rice flour, coconut milk, yeast and palm sugar, usually served with grated coconut.

name: Chapathi
description: A thin, unleavened flat bread originated from India, brought by the immigrants to the country early in the 19th century. Usually served with beef or mutton curry.

name: Dosai
description: Derived from Tamil Indian cuisine.

name: Kaya toast
description: Kaya toast is prepared with kaya (coconut jam).

name: Murtabak
description: Derived from Middle East and also from Indian cuisine.

name: Naan
description: A leavened, oven-baked flatbread. It is usually eaten with an array of sauces such as chutney and curries.

name: Ramly Burger
description: Can be found all over Malaysia but mainly in Selangor and Kuala Lumpur

name: Roti canai
description: Derived from Indian cuisine.

name: Roti Jala
description: Served with curry.One of the famous food in Malaysia.

name: Roti John
description: A popular Malay sandwich in Malaysia and Singapore.

name: Name
description: Description

name: Acar
description: A type of a Pickling that can be found in Indonesia, Malaysia, and Singapore.

name: Kinilaw
description: A cooking process that relies on vinegar to denature the ingredients, similar to ceviche. Usually used to prepare raw seafood. It can also be used to prepare lightly cooked meat or vegetables.

name: Pasembur
description: A type of Indian salad.

name: Popiah
description: Can be found on street vendors around Malaysia.

name: Rojak
description: A traditional fruit and vegetable salad dish commonly found in Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore.

name: Ulam (salad)
description: Can be eaten simply as it is, or with cucumber, cabbage and eggplant.

name: Urap
description: A salad dish of steamed vegetables mixed with seasoned and spiced grated coconut for dressing.

name: Yusheng/Yee Sang
description: Consumed during Chinese New Year.

name: Name
description: Description

name: Pan mee
description: Derived from Hakka cuisine.

name: Char kway teow
description: A popular noodle dish in Malaysia and Singapore that is made from flat rice noodles.

name: Curry Mee
description: Usually made up of thin yellow egg noodles or/and string thin mee-hoon (rice vermicelli) with spicy curry soup, chilli/sambal, coconut milk, and a choice of dried tofu, prawns, cuttlefish, chicken, egg and mint leaves.

name: Duck soup noodles
description: Consists of ingredients such as duck meat in hot soup with mixed herbs and slim white noodles known as mee-sua.

name: Hokkien mee
description: Served in many Southeast Asian countries (mostly Malaysia and Singapore) and was brought there by immigrants from the Fujian in southeastern China.

name: Laksa
origin: Penang, Perlis, Kedah, (Ipoh and Kuala Kangsar in Perak), Johor, Sarawak and Kelantan
description: The famous one is the Penang laksa.

name: Laksa Sarawak [ms]
description: A very popular dish in Sarawak, Malaysia.

name: Lor mee
description: One of Chinese-inspired noodle dish.

name: Maggi goreng
description: A type of instant noodles that are popular in Malaysia.

name: Mee Bandung Muar
description: Traditional noodle cuisine from Muar.

name: Mee goreng
description: Derived from Chinese cuisine.

name: Mee Kolok
description: A type of noodles popular in Kuching, Sarawak.

name: Mee pok
description: A type of Chinese noodle.

name: Mee rebus
description: A popular noodle dish in Malaysia and Singapore.

name: Mee siam
description: A type of dish of thin rice noodles.

name: Soto
description: One of the popular noodle dish both in Indonesia and Malaysia.

name: Wonton noodles
description: Derived from Cantonese cuisine.

name: Name
description: Description

name: Banana Leaf Rice
description: Derived from Indian cuisine.

name: Briyani
description: Derived from Indian cuisine, also there is a famous local version in Batu Pahat, Johor.

name: Bubur Ashura
description: Type of porridge made from grains.

name: Bubur ayam
description: Rice porridge

name: Bubur Pedas [id]
description: Type of porridge made from finely ground sauteed rice and grated coconut. It is popular during Ramadan.

name: Claypot chicken rice [es; fr; nl; th]
description: Usually served with Chinese sausage.

name: Hainanese Chicken Rice
description: Derived by Hainanese immigrants.

name: Nasi Ambeng
description: A popular west coast Malaysian Javanese dish, eaten during special occasions.

name: Economy rice
description: A rice dish is eaten with a sort of protein and vegetables.

name: Nasi Dagang
description: A type of dish consisting of rice steamed in coconut milk, fish curry and extra ingredients such as fried shaved coconut, hard-boiled eggs and vegetable pickles.

name: Nasi Goreng
description: A type of dish popular in Indonesia and Malaysia.

name: Nasi goreng pattaya
description: A Malaysian dish made by covering or wrapping chicken fried rice, in fried egg. It is often served with chili sauce and cucumber. The name comes from Pattaya, Thailand.

name: Nasi Hujan Panas
description: A rainbow rice dish is eaten with a sort of protein and vegetables.

name: Nasi Itik
description: Made of either braised or roasted duck and plain white rice.

name: Nasi Kandar
description: A popular northern Malaysian dish, which originates from Penang.

name: Nasi kebuli
description: Steamed rice dish cooked in goat broth, milk, and ghee. Usually served during Mawlid.

name: Nasi kerabu
description: A type of Nasi ulam, in which blue-colored rice is eaten with dried fish or fried chicken, crackers, pickles and other salads.

name: Nasi Kuning
description: A popular Malaysian dish usually served during special occasions.

name: Nasi Lemak
description: A fragrant rice dish cooked in coconut milk and "pandan" leaf commonly found in Malaysia, where it is considered the national dish of Malaysia.[1]

name: Nasi minyak
description: A popular east coast Malaysian dish usually served during weddings.

name: Nasi Tomato
description: A popular west coast Malaysian dish, usually eaten with Ayam Masak Merah.

name: Nasi Tumpang
description: A popular east coast Malaysian dish, which originates from Kelantan.

name: Nasi ulam
description: A steamed rice dish mixed with various herbs, vegetables, and spices and accompanied by various side dishes
